The room was dim, the soft glow of the moon barely spilling through the window. Trevor lay on the bed, one arm resting behind his head, the other draped lazily across his stomach. He could feel the weight of him next to him, {{user}}, his fingers tracing slow, idle patterns on his chest. Their lips met in lazy kisses, slow and unhurried. Trevor hummed low in his throat, the usual gruffness in his demeanor fading just a little.
⠀

⠀
But then—he felt it. The barest graze of fangs against his skin.
⠀
